topic.what: Maastricht VI/Florence Consensus Report updating European H. pylori management guidelines with new evidence on vonoprazan-based regimens, precision susceptibility-guided therapy, microbiome impact of eradication, and population-level screen-and-treat strategies for gastric cancer prevention.
topic.how: Delphi-method consensus among international experts with systematic review and GRADE-quality evidence rating, organized across updated working groups covering diagnostics, treatment, cancer prevention, microbiota, and special populations.
topic.why_matters: Represents the current state-of-the-art in H. pylori management, incorporating vonoprazan as a PPI alternative, emphasizing precision antibiotic selection over empirical therapy, and strengthening the case for population-wide screen-and-treat programs.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree to which vonoprazan-based regimens are incorporated into guideline recommendations
characteristics.value: Vonoprazan recognized as superior acid suppressant for eradication regimens; recommended where available
characteristics.vs_baseline: Maastricht V did not include vonoprazan (not yet widely available in 2017)
characteristics.evidence: RCTs comparing vonoprazan-triple vs PPI-triple therapy
characteristics.context: Vonoprazan approved in Japan (2014) and expanding globally

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recommendation strength for susceptibility-guided vs empirical therapy
characteristics.value: Strong recommendation for susceptibility-guided therapy when molecular or culture-based resistance data available
characteristics.vs_baseline: Maastricht V relied more heavily on empirical regional resistance data
characteristics.evidence: Meta-analyses showing higher eradication rates with susceptibility-guided treatment
characteristics.context: Particularly relevant for clarithromycin and levofloxacin resistance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Strength of recommendation for population-level H. pylori screening
characteristics.value: Screen-and-treat recommended in populations with high gastric cancer incidence
characteristics.vs_baseline: Maastricht V had more cautious language on population screening
characteristics.evidence: Cost-effectiveness analyses and pilot screening programs
characteristics.context: Implementation varies by national healthcare infrastructure and cancer incidence

not_for: Settings without access to susceptibility testing
Regions where vonoprazan is unavailable and alternatives suffice
requires: Molecular or culture-based susceptibility testing infrastructure (ideal)
Access to vonoprazan or equivalent potassium-competitive acid blockers
Population cancer incidence data for screen-and-treat decisions

novelty: First Maastricht consensus to incorporate vonoprazan-based regimens
Shift from empirical to precision susceptibility-guided therapy as preferred approach
Strengthened screen-and-treat recommendation for high-incidence populations
Expanded microbiome section addressing long-term consequences of eradication on gut ecology
Addressed emerging concern of increasing antibiotic resistance globally
open_problems: Vonoprazan availability and cost remain barriers outside Japan and select markets
Molecular susceptibility testing not widely available in resource-limited settings
Optimal screen-and-treat age and frequency unresolved
Long-term microbiome recovery after eradication therapy incompletely characterized
Whether eradication benefits diminish after advanced precancerous lesions (point of no return)
